name: @radzor/event-bus
description: In-process typed publish/subscribe event bus. Decouples components without external infrastructure. Supports wildcard patterns, async handlers, error isolation, and event history replay.

inputs.name: maxListeners
inputs.description: Maximum listeners per event before emitting a warning.

inputs.name: historySize
inputs.description: Number of past events to retain for late subscribers (0 = no history).

inputs.name: asyncHandlers
inputs.description: Await async handlers sequentially. Set to false for fire-and-forget.

outputs.name: publishResult
outputs.description: Publish result with the event name and number of handlers invoked.

events.name: onPublish
events.description: Fired after an event is published and all handlers complete.

events.name: onHandlerError
events.description: Fired when a handler throws. Other handlers continue to run.

actions.name: subscribe
actions.description: Subscribe to an event. Returns an unsubscribe function.

actions.name: once

params.name: event
params.description: Event name to listen for once.

actions.description: Subscribe to an event for a single invocation, then auto-unsubscribe.

actions.name: publish

params.name: payload
params.description: Event payload passed to all handlers.

actions.description: Publish an event and invoke all matching handlers.

actions.name: unsubscribeAll

params.name: event
params.description: Event name to clear all handlers for.

actions.description: Remove all handlers for an event, or all handlers globally if no event given.

llm.constraints: In-process only — events do not cross process or service boundaries. For distributed systems, use a message queue (@radzor/queue-worker or @radzor/background-job). Handler errors are isolated — one failing handler does not prevent others from running.
